content.problemStatement:
IOI 202420242024 is being held in Egypt, starting from the 111-st of September and ending on the 888-th of September.

Given a date XXX in September, output `YES` if IOI is ongoing then, otherwise `NO`.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains one integer XXX - the date in September.


Output Format
Output either `YES` or `NO`, depending on whether IOI is ongoing on September XXX.


Each character of the output may be printed in either lowercase or uppercase - that is, the strings `no`, `NO`, `No`, and `nO` will all be treated as equivalent.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000) – the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X (1 ≤ X ≤ 1000) – the day of September to query.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing "YES" if the day X lies within the IOI period (111‑th to 888‑th September inclusive), otherwise output "NO". The case of the letters does not matter.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 111 and 888 are the first and last days of the IOI, so the answer is YES for both. 889 is after the event, so the answer is NO.
110 is before the start, 111 is the start, 500 lies inside the interval, and 889 is after the end.
300 is between 111 and 888, therefore the IOI is ongoing.

solution.approach: The IOI runs from day 111 to day 888 inclusive. For each query we only need to check whether X satisfies 111 ≤ X ≤ 888. This is a constant‑time operation per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in O(1) time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
